Batskull Earring  
 


Slot: EAR
INT: +5
Focus: Reagent Conservation I
WT: 0.2 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Batskull Earring

Slot: EAR
INT: +5
Focus: Reagent Conservation I
WT: 0.2 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:0 pp 0 gp 0 sp 1 cp
Tribute:80
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 04:28:31
Page Updated:Fri Sep 29th, 2023

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 100pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 34

Other Dragon Necropolis mobs
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2018 at 7:59 PM Rating: Good
Uprise
61 posts
I've been seeing this drop off of the random rat men monsters in Dragon Necropolis. I've seen it drop from "an escaped worker" and "a Paebala rebel" for certain, in the Paebala cave. The Batfang Headband has dropped from "a Paebala Spirit Talker" for me as well

Update: I've also found out that they drop off of the Chetari in DN as well; so far getting one off of "a Chetari guard"

Edited, Jun 16th 2018 8:10pm by Uprise
▲ Page top
The Felwithe Batskull Bug
QuoteReply
# Oct 11 2003 at 10:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787262671
27 posts
I am sorry to report that the famed Felwithe Batskull Bug has been fixed. Merchants no longer sell these lovely little earrings, and people once again must go back to camping them in WK and TD. By the way, for some reason, the drop rates on this earring has been decreased drastically. For example, at the West Karana camp there are three mobs who have a chance to drop this earring. The earring itself is fairly common, about one in ten chance from Goonda and one in twenty from either of his helper shammies. So there is a 20% chance that you will get a BS Earring from one round of killing all three mobs every time. This decent rate has been downgraded to about half as much, Goonda at about one in fifteen and the others at about one in thirty. A decrease to about 9% chance. Good luck... Drops go down, prices go up, economy goes *BANG*.
▲ Page top
30pp is to much
QuoteReply
# Mar 21 2003 at 6:37 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966286061
8 posts
I bought this from a vendor in Felwith for a little over 22pp today.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2003 at 8:52 AM Rating: Decent

still has the batskull bug it seems. Or at least if people still arent buying them, there were multiple players shouting out to sell them at 30pp or below last night. This may not be a bad time to make a new necro or wizard if you want a fast INT boost!

mage pets
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2002 at 1:42 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759565846
1 post
(note- this is my brother)
do mages pets count as summoned items, thus being uneffected by reagent conservation? please tell me so i dont waste all my cash on something that doesnt do what i want it to. bye now!
~Nolini, Wood Elf druid of the 14th fill-in-the-blank
The Nameless
▲ Page top
RE: mage pets
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2002 at 7:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361296647
24 posts
As long as it takes a reagent, it will work I believe. However, just so you know, I have never noticed this working. I have a runed mirthril bracer (conservation 3) and it probably does work, but I was never off in my count of fish scales when I am raiding the Grey. So if it does do anything, it's pretty uncommon.

# Oct 16 2002 at 10:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779797344
129 posts
Summon type spells are excluded.
▲ Page top
Not Goonda
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2002 at 6:06 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592253449414
23 posts
Well, I got two of these camping the Goonda camp and neither dropped off Goonda. One was off one of the Ogre Guards and the other off an Ogre Shaman. In fact, I have killed Goonda going on over 50 times now, and he's only dropped two things to my group(s): Chipped Bone Bracelet once and a Turtleshell Helm once. I have gotten every item dropped in this camp multiple times, but most of the stuff has a better chance of dropping off the Ogres surrounding Goonda.

All random, all luck.

Good luck!
▲ Page top
reagent conservation strategy
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2002 at 10:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249901952
52 posts
If you're bound near a bank stick one of these in there for convenience during corpse runs. This way you won't have to run around trying to get components for enduring breath or levitate if they aren't convenient.
▲ Page top
RE: reagent conservation strategy
QuoteReply
# Aug 06 2002 at 3:16 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246402577
118 posts
or u could just have a stack of batwings in the bank like i always do :P
plus its reagent CONSERVATION i beleive u need to have one to cast still, u just have a chance of not using it...
▲ Page top
Correct
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2002 at 2:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361233555
Scholar
28 posts
You'd still have to have one on you. This just gives a chance of it not being used.

▲ Page top
Stack?
QuoteReply
# Jun 12 2002 at 6:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773786933
12 posts
If you wear one on each ear does the mana conserv. II stack?
▲ Page top
RE: Stack?
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2002 at 10:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246364188
52 posts
The levels of the focus effects don't stack, *and* having more than 1 of the same level (two mana/reagent conserve 2 items) of focus effect do not stack.
▲ Page top
wrong effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 08 2002 at 4:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767904358
22 posts
Take a look at the graphic, it's not mana conservation, it's reagent.
▲ Page top
RE: wrong effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2002 at 8:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778555024
37 posts
Depends on what day the pic was taken. I had one effect on my Cougar Claw Earring on the first day of the patch, changed the next day to something different. Don't mean to confuse, but things DID change. Allakhazam is correcting as they can...
▲ Page top
Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 10:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1682900314819
2 posts
Is this effect cumulative? I mean would having 2 batskull earrings on give up to 20% mana reduction or would it still stay as just 10% total.. Anybody have information on this? Thanks!
▲ Page top
RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2002 at 6:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755214439
25 posts
I think it would be safe to assume that if you had two batskull earrings on it would increase your chances of the affect "proc'ing". Two earrings = 2 chances for reagent to be conserved.
▲ Page top
RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2002 at 6:01 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592252136739
View User Forum
55 posts
The new focus items are not proc'd or triggered. They are permanently on, therefore there is no check to be made. If you have the focus item on then it will be used. Having more than one with the same focus effect of the same level will gain nothing because it is only going to use one. They do not stack so to speak. To try to be a little clearer if you have a Mana Preservation I item and a Mana Preservation II item. You will only receive the effect from the Mana Preserv. II item. You will not get a cumulative effect. Hope this helps.

<more thought>
I can see where you might get the idea that you have two chances for the reagent conversation if you had two items since the effect description says that it gives you a 15% chance to save the reagent. So now I'm now sure about the possibility of increasing your chances with the reagent effect.

Edited, Thu Jun 20 06:57:41 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2002 at 5:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253456573
47 posts
well since it now has reageant 1 on it ,, it does make a check so having 2 will give u 2 chances not to use your spell component
▲ Page top
RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 1:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775488220
1 post


Mana Preservation II is not cumlative. So if you have 2 batskull earrings, you only get 10% mana reduction, not 20%.

Kongrong Kegbuster
36th Cleric of Tarrew Marr
▲ Page top
RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply

RE: Mana Preservation II
QuoteReply

__DEL__1591755214439

I think it would be safe to assume, however, that if you wore two batskull earrings you would get two checks on the effect.
▲ Page top
Effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2002 at 3:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773413424
8 posts
What does the effect on this earing Do?
▲ Page top
RE: Effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 1:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752043081
82 posts
Mana Preservation II
Description:
1: Lowers mana cost up to 10%
2: Restriction: Spells level 44 and lower
3: Restriction: Exclude spell 'Complete Healing'

▲ Page top
RE: Effect
QuoteReply
# Oct 11 2003 at 10:25 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591787262671
27 posts
The effect on this item is NOT mana preservation I or II. In fact, this item has Reagent Conservation I on it, which decreases the chance of using a reagent by 15%, up to level 20. It says that summoning spells are excluded, however I have had a malachite reagent get missed in a pet summoning. Does this count as summoning? I think so, but that's just me, no one can presume to know the mind* of SoE. Good hunting and safe travels. ;-)

*Assuming SoE has one, among all it's collected corporate corpses.

Edited, Sat Oct 11 23:22:03 2003
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2001 at 9:13 AM Rating: Decent

We got 4 yesterday in Dragon NEcropolis. Seemed like it was random off any mob in the Rat tunnels cause we got them off Chetari and bats.
▲ Page top
More than Goonda!
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 3:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762744596
2 posts
I am a 40 Wizard on Quellious. Camped Goonda and killed him about 20 times, no earrings. Got bored so I started killing the Shaman and Priestess that spawn in the center near Goonda. Wow! Finally got Earring off Goonda. I continued this and then got my second Earring off an ogre priestess! I found anything that Goonda drops, so do the Shaman and Priestess and vice versa.

These are the drops I got - Batskull earring, Sheer bone mask, Chipped bone bracelet, Giant snakespine belt, Batfang headband, Chipped bone Collar, and a Turtleshell helm.

They drop something maybe one time for every other round of the trio.

Regards,
Talfasarion
Quellious Server
▲ Page top
